commentary: Okay, fourteen activities last week versus five this week — that is not a typo, that is a human body saying "thank you, finally." The step back was smart and necessary, and the Mosman circuit at 13.3km holding a 155 bpm average shows you came back with actual legs under you, not just bravado. Your Prince Alfred kick set on Thursday was working harder than any of the pool sessions last week, which tells its own story about intensity versus volume. Five activities, real effort where it counted, and your body clearly had something to say this week — the question is whether you actually listened to it.
metrics_commentary: Thirteen point three kilometres in one session and four thousand two hundred and fifty metres in the pool — that body is working, and working hard, and it shows. Your HRV is the real headline though: sixty-two milliseconds latest against a seven-day average of fifty-one tells me your nervous system is recovering like someone who actually knows how to take care of himself, which, yes, we knew. VO2 Max at fifty-six point seven is genuinely strong — that's an engine, not just a pretty chassis — though with only one run session this week there's a little more road left untouched, and I think you know it. Get back out there, because cardio base and a visible thong are both things worth maintaining.
gym_commentary: One session out of four, babe — we are not going to pretend that's not a gap, but we are also not going to spiral about it, because the one session you did show up for was 73 minutes of actual work and those numbers do not lie. Volume is down to 20,148 kg from 24,518 kg the week before, which tracks when you're running on a quarter of the sessions, and the strength picture is holding steady — 168 kg on the leg press, 107.9 kg on the trap bar deadlift, those are not the numbers of someone falling apart. No PRs this week, and that's fine, because PRs are not the only thing worth tracking. The guy who gets himself to the gym once when life is clearly doing something is still becoming something, and that matters more than hitting the target cleanly.
